reasoning: In Munkres' textbook, a "regular" space is defined to include T1 (singletons closed) plus the separation axiom for points and closed sets. The Lean signature correctly encodes this as `[RegularSpace X] [T1Space X]`, which together is equivalent to Munkres' notion of regular (i.e., T3). The conclusion asks for open neighborhoods U of x and V of y with disjoint closures, which matches "neighborhoods whose closures are disjoint" (an open neighborhood is a neighborhood, and any neighborhood contains an open one, so the existential form is equivalent). The added hypothesis `x ≠ y` is necessary and implicit in the informal phrase "pair of points" — if x = y, disjoint closures would be impossible. Thus the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
